title: centaurea alba subsp. deusta
intro: Centaurea alba subsp. deusta, also known as the White Cockle or Corn Cockle, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally used in herbal medicine, it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and act as an antiseptic to prevent infections. Centaurea alba subsp. deusta is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
